county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: West Virginia county; originally Virginia county formed 1845; became part of WV at statehood 1863
county_metadata.research_tips: WV Archives (Charleston) and Gilmer County Clerk (Glenville). Formed 1845 from Lewis and Kanawha. Glenville State University archives have some local materials. Pre-1863 records at LVA Richmond. Small, rural county. Oil and gas development from the 1860s. Gilmer County Historical Society. Check Lewis and Kanawha for pre-1845 records. Cedar Creek area families documented in early land grants.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: WV Archives microfilm and county clerk records; pre-1863 at LVA

county_metadata.research_tips: WV Archives (Charleston) and Grant County Clerk (Petersburg). Formed 1866 (post-statehood) from Hardy County -- one of WV's youngest counties. All formation records in Hardy County. South Branch Potomac Valley -- German and Scots-Irish settlement from 1740s. Smoke Hole area families documented in early land patents. Grant County Historical Society. Timber industry important in late 19th century. Good surviving records from formation.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: West Virginia county; originally Virginia county formed 1778; became part of WV at statehood 1863; coal mining region
county_metadata.research_tips: WV Archives (Charleston) and Greenbrier County Clerk (Lewisburg). Major county formed 1778 from Montgomery (VA) and Botetourt (VA). Lewisburg is a historic town with excellent surviving records. The Greenbrier resort area -- historic hotel records. Pre-1863 records at LVA Richmond. German and Scots-Irish settlers predominated. Greenbrier Historical Society has exceptional genealogical collections. Civil War battle at Lewisburg (1862). Enslaved population documented in tax lists and estate records. Timber and railroad development in late 19th century.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: WV Archives microfilm and county clerk records; pre-1863 at LVA

county_metadata.record_losses: Civil War damage; courthouse raided multiple times 1861-1865; some records lost or scattered
county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: West Virginia county; originally Virginia county formed 1754; became part of WV at statehood 1863
county_metadata.research_tips: WV Archives (Charleston) and Hampshire County Clerk (Romney). BURNED/DAMAGED -- Civil War damage; courthouse raided multiple times during the war. One of WV's oldest counties, formed 1754 from Frederick (VA) and Augusta (VA). Romney changed hands 56 times during the Civil War. Pre-1863 records at LVA Richmond (especially important due to war damage). Hampshire County is the oldest county in WV. German and Scots-Irish settlement from 1740s. Fort Edwards and other colonial frontier records. Hampshire County Committee for the Arts has local materials. Indian Old Fields archaeological site.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: WV Archives microfilm and county clerk records; pre-1863 at LVA

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: West Virginia county; originally Virginia county formed 1848; became part of WV at statehood 1863; Northern Panhandle
county_metadata.research_tips: WV Archives (Charleston) and Hancock County Clerk (New Cumberland). Northern Panhandle county formed 1848 from Brooke. WV's smallest county by area and northernmost point. Major pottery and ceramics industry -- Homer Laughlin China Company (Newell). Chester and Newell industrial records. Pre-1863 records at LVA Richmond. Check Brooke County for pre-1848 records. Cross-reference with Ohio and Pennsylvania records due to Panhandle location. Good surviving records.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: WV Archives microfilm and county clerk records; pre-1863 at LVA

county_metadata.record_losses: Civil War destruction; 1863 partial damage to records; some gaps in deed and court books
county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: West Virginia county; originally Virginia county formed 1786; became part of WV at statehood 1863
county_metadata.research_tips: WV Archives (Charleston) and Hardy County Clerk (Moorefield). BURNED/DAMAGED -- Civil War destruction caused some record losses. Formed 1786 from Hampshire. South Branch Valley -- German settlement from 1740s (Van Meter and other early families). Pre-1863 records at LVA Richmond are critical due to war damage. Moorefield was an important Valley town. Hardy County Historical Society. Enslaved population documented. Fort Pleasant and other frontier fort records. Poultry industry significant in 20th century. Check Hampshire for pre-1786 records.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: WV Archives microfilm and county clerk records; pre-1863 at LVA

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: West Virginia county; originally Virginia county formed 1784; became part of WV at statehood 1863
county_metadata.research_tips: WV Archives (Charleston) and Harrison County Clerk (Clarksburg). Major county formed 1784 from Monongalia. Clarksburg was an important early town -- Stonewall Jackson's birthplace. Pre-1863 records at LVA Richmond. Good surviving records. Harrison County Historical Society has local materials. Oil and gas industry from the 1860s. B&O Railroad hub. WVU Regional History Center has Harrison County materials. Check Monongalia for pre-1784 records. Enslaved population documented in early records.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: WV Archives microfilm and county clerk records; pre-1863 at LVA

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: West Virginia county; originally Virginia county formed 1831; became part of WV at statehood 1863; Ohio River county
county_metadata.research_tips: WV Archives (Charleston) and Jackson County Clerk (Ripley). Formed 1831 from Mason, Kanawha, and Wood. Ohio River county with good surviving records. Pre-1863 records at LVA Richmond. Ravenswood and Ripley area records. Jackson County Historical Society. Check parent counties for earlier records. Salt industry significant in early period. Civil War activity in the county. FamilySearch has microfilmed deed and marriage records.

county_metadata.record_losses: Civil War damage; courthouse used as barracks and hospital; some records lost or damaged 1861-1865
county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: West Virginia county; originally Virginia county formed 1801; became part of WV at statehood 1863; Eastern Panhandle
county_metadata.research_tips: WV Archives (Charleston) and Jefferson County Clerk (Charles Town). BURNED/DAMAGED -- Civil War damage; courthouse used as barracks. Formed 1801 from Berkeley. John Brown's raid on Harpers Ferry (1859) -- extensive records at Harpers Ferry NHP and NARA. Eastern Panhandle county with significant enslaved population. Pre-1863 records at LVA Richmond. German and Scots-Irish settlement. Jefferson County Historical Society and Museum. Shepherdstown (oldest town in WV) area records. Washington family (George Washington's relatives) had extensive holdings. Harpers Ferry National Historical Park archives.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: WV Archives microfilm and county clerk records; pre-1863 at LVA

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: West Virginia county; originally Virginia county formed 1789; became part of WV at statehood 1863; coal mining region
county_metadata.research_tips: WV Archives (Charleston) and Kanawha County Clerk (Charleston). WV's most populous county, formed 1789 from Greenbrier and Montgomery (VA). Charleston is the state capital. Major parent county for many WV counties. Pre-1863 records at LVA Richmond. Salt industry was the first major industry -- Kanawha Salines records. Chemical industry (Union Carbide, DuPont) records important for 20th century. WV Archives and History Library is IN this county, making it the most accessible for research. Kanawha Valley Historical and Preservation Society. Enslaved workers documented in salt industry records.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: WV Archives microfilm and county clerk records; pre-1863 at LVA

source_profiles.source_name: Kanawha County Civil War Records -- Union and Confederate
source_profiles.repository: WV Archives and History Library / NARA / LVA
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Both Union and Confederate service records for Kanawha County area; WV was a divided state with men serving on both sides

source_profiles.source_name: Kanawha County Coal Company and Mine Records
source_profiles.repository: WVU West Virginia and Regional History Center / Eastern Regional Coal Archives
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Coal company records, employee lists, company town records, mine inspection reports for Kanawha County; essential for tracing mining families
